reviews.review_title: Still a work in progress people
reviews.review_description: I have played a ton of games for the past 30 years, the concept it seems the creators are going for is to give everyone something to do and tailored across the board for the various concepts they enjoy. They have incorporated a mix of talent trees or professions for people such as platform dynamics, FPS, sword and sorcery styles and archery. Yeah there are bugs but the game isnt comleted. There are going to be bugs in any game thats still being worked on such as screen tearing, glitching npcs and world surroundings, etc. You want a completed game buy one. You want to watch a game take life play one in early access or beta testing. However all being said this game has great potential to be epic, but people need to be patient and give it a change to get past developement and the kinks worked out of the system. Even for a early access game I have enjoyed it greatly and normally i go looking for issues in this stuff. Gives great references and such for those who grew up in the 80s and 90s becoming adults in the early 2000s and the players who are there are all about helping new people learn to play. Just know going in its gonna have some issues until completion so for the people giving bad pr about it stop. Your are judging a game thats not completed. Go just and gripe about flaws on other stuff like DOE or Guardians frontline. At least those are completed games with issues and bugs that you ahve to pay for that arent free.

reviews.review_title: tl;dr it's not good and never will be
reviews.review_description:
This review is basically a rant, so.. Be Warned!
 I'm genuinely embarrassed to have played this game for as long as I did. This "game" is free unity asset hell with AI that tries to 'create' NPCs, images and possibly weapons. You GRIND for HOURS doing mind numbing tasks like punching or playing VR Fall Guys so then you have the privilege to GRIND for more HOURS (thank you horrible RNG) for equipment so you use that equipment to GRIND bosses that you probably can't solo. And that's literally it, yeah there's social environments but so is any game if it has at least a crouch button. Gameplay aside I'd like to talk about presentation. It's ugly and poorly optimized, classic "I'm an amateur game dev way in over my head and made something too big for myself to handle but I don't want to abandon this terrible project" stuff, reminds me of DreamWorld. It's also one of those "metaverse" things so "Look Star Wars! Now you're in a haunted witch house! Now you're in a bright baby blue Roblox obby jumping on bright pink!" The vibes are all over the place and nowhere looks good because the level design is horrible, copy and paste stuff. This game comes across as lazy, using free (or possibly stolen but I doubt that) assets and AI to make a game for them. Then they have players grind for tens of hours and put them in this Stockholm Syndrome loop of thinking the game is good actually; just spend the $30 and buy Dungeons Of Eternity already.

reviews.review_title: This could be so much better....
reviews.review_description:
1.   There are no creating tools as of yet nor any way to get them.  Therefore the "creator tools" tab at the start menu is effectively useless.

2.  The XP/Skills gain system is not well documented (if there is documentation I haven't found it) or developed.   There is no clear way on how one is to select or assign skills.  Nor is there ANY documentation on this subject.

3.   Documentation is sparse at best.   This includes item and gear acquisition, places to grind mobs, how to edit items, skills/XP, and proper avatar use (what happens when you drop a weapon?  How do you properly holster a weapon?  How do you put stuff in your inv etc?). None of this is documented.

Rift has dozens of user made mods, levels and avatars but most are only half done.   This game has so much potential but it needs so much work to be a real functional platform for user generated content.

game has good ideas, good MMORPG aspects, ai bots and ai image generation. i understand you need to attract more players but youre unable too... tried to show this game to several people and they all stopped playing..   why make these bad graphic games in 2025 and wonder why nobody plays... its UGLY... SOME 1 GOTTA BE TRUTHFUL... ALSO whatever graphics overhaul you did... made it less smoothe gameplay and still ugly 🤣, its studdery on quest 3....should of left this on pc.... its obvious that it doesn't work here.. this my first time ever given a low review 💯 its too obvious

Riff has grown so much since it started! The community base is amazing, with people willing to help wherever you go. There are guild, solo battles, grinding, quests,l and missions, whatever your heart desires. My guild <TSM> is one of the best out there, with a ton of people who are there to have fun, help each other, and play with other people on the game. Whether you need help with a boss fight, to kill a few zombies, tag some dread bears in the Mansion, trade for gear, or fight hellcats, all you have to do is ask. And, the DEV is in the game playing with us and listening to us regularly! What other game has all of that? Riff XR is the best!

Good

I recently tried Rift XR, and while the game shows a lot of potential, the experience has been disappointing for several reasons.

First off, the hit registration is bad, which makes gameplay frustrating and inconsistent. There’s no party system, so theres no Exp share with friends and part calls are unnecessarily difficult. With that being said The in-game calling system is clunky and the calls aren’t private everyone can hear you still, and navigating the menu feels awkward. While it’s not hard to understand, it’s far from smooth, much like the controls and the worlds in the game. On top of that, the game wastes your time in ways that feel avoidable. Whether it’s the awkward design choices or the lack of basic features, these frustrations add up quickly.

Additionally, my friends and I joined their Discord to offer feedback, hoping to help improve the game. Sadly, these suggestions weren’t well received. The community also came off as quite toxic, dismissing ideas and making unfounded accusations. Suggestions like adopting controls to help VRChat players transition more easily were met with hostility. The developers seemed defensive rather than open to constructive criticism, which made it hard to engage in any meaningful way.

What’s worse is how passive-aggressive the developers and community can be. At first, they act nice, but if you end up on their bad side, they quickly become hostile and aggressive one of the community members threatened my friend by saying “I hope you’re using a VPN” clearly threatening to Dox my friend. It’s a frustrating dynamic that discourages anyone from giving feedback or getting involved.

What’s frustrating is that in-game, we’ve been praised by other users for our suggestions. Many told us they agreed 100% with what we were saying but were too nervous to bring it up to the developers themselves. This shows there’s a real need for better communication and openness from the developers.

A community can make or break a game, and unfortunately, Rift XR’s community leans toward the latter. It’s not very welcoming to people who think differently or don’t act in unison with the group. Instead of embracing diverse attitudes and ideas, they’re often hostile to newcomers who don’t conform to their way of doing things.

Rift XR has potential, but it needs better hit detection, smoother menus and controls, and a more welcoming and constructive community if it wants to attract and retain players.

reviews.review_title: This game is a complete mess.
reviews.review_description:
It's got potential but this is not a game yet. It's a collection of game assets to outline how a game should look before they commit to making anything. Your first hour will see you talking to an AI that constantly hallucinates buildings and objectives that don't exist. If you ask it "where can I get new weapons" you will get a response that mianders for 3-4 minutes and only says "go to the blacksmith in town" problems the town doesn't exist and neither does the blacksmith. You only get new weapons from farming the most basic AI monsters. Or you can partner up with several other players who don't exist because the game peaks at 12 players online.

The games first challenge is fighting "The Glitch" not only is the glitch impossible for any new player to defeat on their own, but it's darn near impossible for any group less than 12-20. Or some half with who is already in the end game can just solo it for you and that's probably the way most players have gotten past "the Glitch". 

The actual gameplay sucks it's a worse version of the Meta verse except you can just have no chance against another player because they are higher level than you. Getting weapons is just never explained to you in game you literally cannot find information on it anywhere within the game. weapons are a rare drop from enemies and bosses. Since the drop rates are so low for just the starting gear you probably won't even think you are doing the right thing to get new weapons.

reviews.review_title: One of the best vr mmorpg experiences!
reviews.review_description:
This game has been one of my favorite vr experiences so far. The graphics are crisp with some beautiful worlds, the skill trees are nice, I love the avatars available and it being open to community created content is great. The community is super helpful. The quests and missions are  very fun and engaging. The AI NPCS are cool to talk to and it also has a giant NPC dungeon master so you can run a full DND campaign with friends!

I can see this being the future of vr where it can be a main hub for everyone to meet and play and build up their character and gain achievements. It's still super early so there isn't a huge player base but that's just because people aren't aware of the game yet.

This game is for anyone that loves VRChat but wants RPG elements like inventories, skills, missions, tournaments. Etc...

If you love social games and mmorpgs, this game is perfect for you.

It's on path to become the real life oasis from ready player one!

I give it an 8/10!

reviews.review_title: It has a lot of potential.
reviews.review_description:
I really want to like this game, but it has some fundamental issues. First and foremost is controls,  they are the most aggravating part of this game. It takes me 6 tries to draw my shotgun, 3 tries to actually register a pump, and if you drop it there's no picking it back up. You have to wait for it to despawn and then redraw it. Using a pistol is no easier, the only consistent way I've been able to reload my pistol is to smack my hand against my chest, and the aim seems severely off. Oh and the holsters are random, doesn't matter which side I reach for, I have a 50/50 chance of drawing either gun I have equipped.

Content: A large portion of the game is currently locked behind social activities, which are hard to do with such a low player count. You need more fun solo material for people to enjoy while they wait for the population count to rise. The only thing I was able to do was shoot some zombies, all other enemies are bullet sponges that one shot me. There's an NPC on the dead wave map that gives quests, but there's no way to complete them. There's very little loot I was able to access, none of it felt like an upgrade (Pistol does 20 damage, shotgun does 25? Where's all the melee weapons I saw in the trailer?), and I can't figure out where to spend the in game currency. Several of the maps, while pretty, are completely devoid of objects, NPCs, enemies or really anything to interact with.

This game seems far too dependent on the grind to maintain engagement and is a skeleton compared to the games that do this successfully, long story short it is a good CONCEPT for a game but the game itself is almost entirely a never ending grind that gets boring the second you break free from the cycle, hell often times even the cycle itself is boring and if you DARE to mention these issues related to being too grindy and reducing the grind to the discord people will either defend the grind or rarely decide just ask what you need and tell you what annoying enemy you need to grind for weeks to get said item.
Only issue I have had is when updates come out it take 12-24 hrs for them to show up

It’s been a month and I have over 300 hours of game play. It’s not easy to find guides but asking others in game on what to do was helpful. Once you reached a mastery it’s fun bossing with a team and able to get better gear as i work on getting better. 

The 4 star it’s because of party call issues.

Just what I have been looking for. The early grind is a grind but then the game opens up all sorts of opportunities. Updates are coming all the time and new content is introduced frequently. The guild system is excellent (mine is TSM) and alliances between guilds are good.

I've never had a game in VR both look so immediately mediocre while also averaging under 10 fps the entire time. I couldn't even change maps because the login lobby was so laggy I almost vommited immediately. This game needs some serious hardcore optimization before it can even remotely be considered usable, much less fun. The aesthetic was cool but this just another rec room or vrc or horizon worlds. Like every unfinished sandbox demo on quest, this game expects the players to make its content for them rather than the devs actually putting work and care into their product. I wouldn't recommend this game to anyone if my life depended on it.

reviews.review_title: Riffs is the best game out there
reviews.review_description: Riffs is the funnest game and a great community of people

Review:

This game is a mess from start to finish. It’s extremely grindy, with monsters that feel like bullet sponges, requiring an absurd amount of effort to kill. To make things worse, the combat system is poorly designed—guns can’t be aimed down sights properly, which makes ranged combat feel clunky and unsatisfying. On top of that, the hit registration with melee weapons like swords is downright horrible, making close combat equally frustrating.

The game markets itself as a VRC competitor but then backpedals on that claim. It also advertises itself as D&D-inspired, but it’s not real D&D. Instead, it’s an AI-generated story called “The Dungeon Master,” which feels like a cheap attempt to capitalize on the D&D name without delivering on its promise. The AI-generated elements also lack safety or moderation, which is a huge oversight.

If you decide to play with a group (which you’ll have to, thanks to the tanky enemies), you’ll quickly realize the party system is fundamentally broken. Only the player who lands the final hit gets experience, meaning everyone else walks away empty-handed. This encourages selfish gameplay and punishes teamwork, completely undermining the idea of cooperative play.

The user interface (UI) is terrible, making even basic interactions frustrating. You can’t block problem players, leaving you vulnerable to harassment or trolling. The game’s visuals are subpar, and the trailers are blatantly misleading. What looked like a game with potential ended up being a complete disappointment.

Final Thoughts:
What could have been an exciting and innovative experience turned out to be nothing more than a frustrating grind-fest with bad graphics, clunky mechanics, a broken party system, and false promises. With terrible UI design and no safeguards on the AI-generated art, this game is not worth anyone’s time. Stick to VRChat or other, better options until something more polished and enjoyable comes along. Save your time and your sanity.

Love Riff, it's the most friendly and engaging thing I've played for years. It is constantly evolving and there's often new content added. I've only been on about a month and already made load of friends and started a second character to experience more of the possible character builds. Totally recommend giving it a go.

Worth every penny at thrice the price!  Join while it's in Early Access & grow with the game!  You won't regret it!

reviews.review_title: Game is garbage, avoid at all cost
reviews.review_description:
From the moment I spawned in, I dealt with nothing but issues. Starting off, what you see in the promo IS NOT the game you get. There's either massive lag spikes or the frame rate is so horribly bad that any time you try moving, there's almost a full second of delay ( tried on multiple wifi providers, this problem exists no matter what), The "a.i" npc's only ever respond to very specific phrases and unless there's someone nearby to tell you what they are, you WILL be stuck at the tutorial with no guidance or directions on how even to do the most basic of actions, and don't even get me started on the beginning mission in the mansion. Guns do next to no damage, enemies move way faster than you can sprint away, and the only players that seem to actually be online ( despite what all these fake positive reviews say, there's next to no one) are those who are specifically wait for a new player to join the beginning mission , just so they can go around and grief them as they struggle to even figure out how to play the game. 

In my honest opinion this couldn't even pass for a pre-alpha and I wouldn't be surprised that if I took a look into the files, all I'd find are stolen assets ( don't believe me? Look at the " zombies" in the haunted mansion)

The creator has done an astounding job and uses player feedback like its gold! Where this game is in development its gonna me righteous when its done! This is not a basic game for basic people, you really gotta put in the time and work to grow and strengthen your character! Go into this game eyes open. It really is as close to ready player one as we can currently get.

reviews.review_title: This game is full of potential!
reviews.review_description:
I have been playing Riff XR on Steam for a while and thought I’d also try the standalone version to see the difference. Apart from lowered details the game runs very smooth and it’s wonderful to see that this brings people together from both standalone and PC VR platforms!

There are several updates a week and content is being added at a steady pace because of this. The game is constantly growing and improving which is great!

The community is also very nice and friendly with more players joining every week.

I cannot wait to see what comes next for the game. There are of course things that can be improved upon but the game is still in early access. The Devs are active both in game and on their discord so you can also hear about what is being worked on and get teasers for future content!

If you like MMO style game with social elements where you can level up different skills and fight monsters with your friends definitely try put Riff XR!

Just what is this game? Like my guy, it's so glitchy, I don't even know what to do. NOT BEGINNER FRIENDLY. I played the game for 3 hours and all I got to learn was how to change my avatar. Game is pretty dead too. On a good day there's only like 3 people, 2 three year olds and a cat who crawled into a headset just to fall asleep. Its free, it should be. I wouldn't waste my time on this game. Its like VR chat and blade and sorcery had a horrible monstrosity of a baby which is this game. I'm sorry but 1/5

If you don't already have Riff Xr you need to download!! It has quickly become another daily game Secrets everywhere and getting stronger with noticeable increase in abilities.  This game is about to shoot for the stars you want to get in now and be there for those updates that reveal amazing additions! I can't wait to see what's up and coming and ideas in the works!

I put an early review of 5 stars on this game when I was only 3 days in. A month later and I’m even more impressed. The team of developers are working ridiculously hard every day giving us updates and making this game more and more magical. The community is amazing it’s not uncommon to see seasoned players in the plaza (game lobby) helping new players. 

This game seems to be the perfect fit for any type of gamer. It has shooting, sword fighting, boxing, magic, even has obstacle courses similar to those of popular game shows like wipeout and ninja warrior.
The large and growing arsenal of weapons is truly an eye opener.
Not just the amount of obtainable weapons but the amount of different weapon types is unreal. 

Prefer realism? Go for an m16 or a UMP.
Star Wars fan? We’ve got different blasters, Han solos pistol etc.
Just been to see deadpool v Wolverine at the cinema? Put on a deadpool suit and grab yourself a couple M9s and katanas.

Also we now have guilds!!! There are multiple amazing guilds available to join (especially Rogue Nation). Some large some small or you can make your own!!!

This game will seriously pull you in and steal your time without you even knowing. 
I am addicted and you will be too.
Come meet everyone in the wonderful world of Riff Xr.

reviews.review_title: Best VR experience I’ve ever had!!!!
reviews.review_description: Riff is any and everything that a game let alone a VR  MMO SHOULD BE!!! Massive maps, amazing community, ACTIVE DEVS, quality content and so much more! For a game in its pre alpha early access state it’s 1000% a high potential game, it’s always updated almost weekly with new content and fixes for any bugs or problems. All around amazing team, game and can’t forget experience, thank you Ron and the dev team for such an amazing game🔥🔥🔥💯🙌🏽

Riff XR is seriously a game-changer, and I’ve been loving it! First off, it’s incredibly easy to get into, whether you’re on PCVR or Meta Quest standalone. The controls are smooth, and everything just works without a faff. What really blew me away is the avatar freedom—being able to customize and create something that feels completely unique to me is brilliant. You can go wild with it, which I love.

The loots are addictive; it’s one of those games where finding new gear genuinely feels rewarding every single time. It’s not just cosmetic either—each item you find makes you want to keep going, and it’s satisfying seeing your progression. Levelling up your profession skills is another highlight for me, especially as I’ve been honing my healer abilities. The system is well-balanced, so you’re always improving and unlocking something new.

What really stands out though is the community. Other players and guilds are extremely friendly and always make you feel welcome. Whether you’re teaming up for a raid or just hanging out, people are always up for a chat or to lend a hand, which makes the whole experience even better.

The world itself gives off serious Ready Player One vibes—massive, immersive, and constantly evolving. Whether you’re battling bots in PvE or hanging out in places like the Metaverse nightclub, the game’s got so much to offer. If you haven’t downloaded it yet, seriously, what are you waiting for? It’s 100% worth it.
